meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Client Workflow to Solve Debugging Hell
content.seo_description: Master advanced debugging workflows as a freelancer. Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to fix complex bugs fast and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: Stop wrestling with opaque bugs by integrating Claude Code into your local environment. Establish a standardized, automated diagnostic loop: ingest logs, isolate the regression, and use Antigravity to validate the fix. This shift turns 'can't debug' into a repeatable, high-margin service deliverable that guarantees code reliability for every client.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is pinging you on Slack, and your terminal is bleeding red stack traces. You are staring at a legacy codebase that feels like a house of cards, and every attempt to fix one bug triggers three more. The crushing weight of 'can't debug' isn't just a technical hurdle; it is a threat to your reputation and your bottom line.

But elite freelancers don't debug by trial and error. They build systems that make bugs visible, traceable, and trivial to squash. By weaponizing modern AI-assisted CLI tools, you can transform your development workflow from a chaotic scramble into a surgical operation. It is time to stop guessing and start engineering with precision.

steps.title: Contextualize the Regression
steps.description: Before touching code, map the failure state. Use Claude Code to ingest the recent commit history and logs to identify exactly when the behavior deviated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided logs and recent git diff. Identify the specific function responsible for the regression and explain the state mismatch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the last 5 logs and compare with the current directory state to isolate the bug.'

steps.title: Automated Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Never fix a bug without a harness. Force the codebase to fail consistently by writing a reproduction test case automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test case that reproduces this specific bug. Ensure the test fails before we apply any fixes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write a failing test case for the identified issue in a new test file.'

steps.title: Surgical Refactoring with Antigravity
steps.description: Apply the fix using Antigravity to ensure the patch respects the architectural constraints of the project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified function to fix the bug while maintaining existing API contracts and performance benchmarks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the refactor suggested by Antigravity and run the test suite.'

steps.title: Dependency Impact Audit
steps.description: Ensure your fix didn't break downstream modules. Run a full dependency audit to check for side effects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current change for potential side effects in downstream modules and suggest necessary interface updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Check for dependency impact and run all related module tests.'

steps.title: Client-Ready Documentation
steps.description: Transform your debugging session into a professional report to prove value to your client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a brief technical summary of the bug, the root cause, and the permanent fix implemented for the client project report.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Summarize the bug fix in a professional Markdown format for client communication.'

pro_tips: Always maintain a clean branch for every specific bug; it allows for atomic reverts if the AI-assisted fix introduces a regression.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to persist project-specific architectural patterns across different debugging sessions.
When debugging production issues, always mask sensitive environment variables and API keys before feeding logs into the AI context window.
Treat your debug logs as telemetry data—periodically run an analysis to find patterns in your bugs to identify if your architecture needs a structural pivot.
Use Antigravity to generate 'negative tests'—tests that verify the system handles unexpected inputs gracefully—to prevent future debugging headaches.
content.next_steps: Implement this workflow on your next client ticket. Once you have a successful fix, document the process into your own internal 'Standard Operating Procedure' to decrease your billable hours per task while increasing your reliability.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your schedule and start shipping with absolute confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
